identity.short_name: Cycle lengths enclosing exactly 4 interior vertices in side-5 triangular grid (sum)

problem_spec.summary: A robot moves between adjacent unit-triangle cells inside a side-5 triangular grid, forming a simple closed path (no backtrack, all visited cells distinct). The bounded region contains a specified number m of interior grid-vertices (where 6 unit triangles meet). Using duality and a perimeter formula k=6m-2e for the boundary length, enumerate the achievable cycle lengths for fixed m and compute their sum.
givens: T is an equilateral triangle of side length 5 partitioned into unit equilateral triangle cells.
Two cells are adjacent iff they share a common edge.
The robot moves between adjacent cells and never immediately backtracks.
A closed path of length k visits exactly k distinct cells (so it is a simple cycle in the adjacency graph).
Interior grid-vertices are vertices of the subdivision not on the boundary of T (six unit triangles meet there).
The region bounded by the cycle contains exactly m=4 interior grid-vertices.
ask: Let S be the set of all k for which such a cycle exists. Compute Σ_{k\inS} k and output it as a three-digit zero-padded number.

parameter(s).name: move_rule
parameter(s).purpose: forbids immediate reversal; with distinct-visit condition yields simple cycles

parameter(s).name: cycle_measure
parameter(s).purpose: identifies simple cycles in the cell adjacency graph

parameter(s).name: enclosed_interior_vertex_count
parameter(s).purpose: fixes m, the number of interior grid-vertices inside the cycle (here 4)

invariant(s).name: dual_graph_bipartite
invariant(s).statement: The cell adjacency graph is bipartite by triangle orientation (up vs down), so every cycle has even length.

invariant(s).name: interior_vertex_hex_face_correspondence
invariant(s).statement: Each interior grid-vertex corresponds to a hexagonal face in the dual graph (six unit triangles surround it). A simple cycle bounds a connected union of these faces.

invariant(s).name: fixed_m_perimeter_formula
invariant(s).statement: If the bounded region contains m interior vertices and the induced adjacency among them has e shared edges, then the boundary cycle length is k = 6m - 2e (each shared edge removes two boundary edges).

invariant(s).name: finite_internal_graph_for_side5
invariant(s).statement: For side_length=5, the interior-vertex adjacency graph has exactly 6 vertices and 9 edges, so for fixed m=4, the possible e values are finite and can be enumerated.

essential_assumption(s): A closed path visiting exactly k distinct cells is a simple cycle in the planar dual graph.
The region bounded by a simple cycle corresponds to a connected union of hexagonal faces (interior vertices).
The perimeter identity k=6m-2e applies to this dual-face union.
uniqueness_certificate(s): For fixed side_length=5 and m=4, every valid cycle length must equal 6m-2e for some connected 4-vertex subgraph of the fixed 6-vertex interior graph, and each achievable e yields an achievable cycle. Enumerating the finite set of possible e values uniquely determines S and hence Σ_{k\inS} k.

technique(s).name: parity_and_cycle_model_in_dual_graph
technique(s).purpose: convert robot motion to cycles in a bipartite graph and restrict k to even values

technique(s).name: internal_vertex_perimeter_model
technique(s).purpose: relate cycle length k to m and internal adjacency edge count e via k=6m-2e

technique(s).name: enumerate_connected_internal_subgraphs_fixed_m
technique(s).purpose: list possible e values for connected m-vertex subgraphs, hence possible k values

technique(s).name: compute_sum_and_format
technique(s).purpose: compute Σ_{k\inS} k and zero-pad to three digits

theorem(s).name: bipartite_graph_cycle_parity
theorem(s).role: all cycles in a bipartite graph have even length

theorem(s).name: planar_boundary_count_for_union_of_hex_faces
theorem(s).role: boundary length = 6m - 2e for a connected union of m hexagons with e shared edges

theorem(s).name: connected_subgraph_edge_bounds
theorem(s).role: a connected graph on m vertices has at least m-1 edges

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: translate constrained motion to planar cycles, then compute possible perimeters via a finite internal-structure count.

critical_trick(s): Use the bipartite nature of the dual adjacency graph to constrain cycle lengths to be even.
Encode the interior of a cycle by the induced adjacency graph on enclosed interior vertices and use k=6m-2e.
For side_length=5 and fixed m=4, enumerate possible edge counts e in the finite 6-vertex internal graph to obtain S and its sum.

library(s).name: networkx
library(s).reason: optional connectedness checks for induced subgraphs

task(s).name: build_internal_graph_side5
task(s).reason: encode the 6 interior vertices and 9 adjacency edges

task(s).name: enumerate_connected_size4_subgraphs
task(s).reason: compute attainable e values and thus attainable k values for m=4

task(s).name: compute_sum_and_zero_pad
task(s).reason: sum k values and format as three digits
